name: Small Functions
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 3: Functions
problem: Functions that span many lines mix multiple levels of abstraction, contain nested control structures, and become difficult to name, test, and understand independently.
steps: Aim for functions that are 4-20 lines long; question any function exceeding 20 lines.
Ensure blocks inside if, else, and while statements are a single function call.
Keep indentation depth at one or two levels maximum.
Extract any logical section or named comment inside a function into its own function.
Name the extracted function so clearly that the original body reads like a high-level outline.
